paragraphs.body: On simple white pieces of paper with black writing nicely printed on the piece of paper to represent what was purchased or done. The receipts always ended up at the bottom of my mother's purse or in my father's pockets at the end of the night. Some receipts smelled like cinnamon and sugar to remind us of the churros that we devoured. Receipts that felt more wrinkled to remind us of the little walking Santa Clauss we got for decorations that got thrown into a bag right away. Throughout the whole night, you constantly heard receipts getting ripped off the machines and handed to people. They represent the quality family time that we all share during this busy time of year with everything going on. Even though the receipts will eventually get thrown away in a couple of months when my mother decides to clean out her purse for the first time in months, they will always hold the valuable memories that we made.

annotations.text: Some receipts smelled like cinnamon and sugar to remind us of the churros that we devoured.

paragraphs.body: It's a cold and dark December night in the city of Philadelphia and we are driving back home from walking around the Christmas Village in Philadelphia where we spent the whole day shopping. We spent the day in Philly and started the day by going to Macy’s to watch the light show in the store and to the Charles Dickens Village which is a family tradition to go every year at Christmas time. We spent the rest of the day looking at all the unique little trinkets that each vendor had and was selling. Look at all the amazing artwork that was being showcased like hand-painted portraits of Eagles like Jason Kelce, Nick Foles, and Brandon Graham and Phillies players like Bryce Harper and Mike Schmidt. Artwork made up of words that outlined famous musicians spanning from 1950s musicians to today's musicians. I ran to this one booth that had one of these beautifully done artworks that resembled The Beatles' Abbey Road and stared at the artwork for some time before my dad told me that we were going to get some churros from a truck on the other side. The food as well was delicious and was well worth waiting in the lines to get the unique sweet treats that the different vendors had. We ended our day at our favorite little restaurant in the city called Smokin’ Betty’s where my mother asked our waiter who the bartender was because depending on who the bartender was, it depended on what she got. At the end of the diner, my dad handed back the receipt to the waiter and we were off back home after a fantastic day in the city with my family.

annotations.comment: Wow! Look at your writing :) You have improved so much!! Fab details :)
annotations.context_left: shopping. We spent the day in Philly and started the day by going to Macy’s to watch the light

paragraphs.body: Family time was always something that my parents cared about but it was always hard to figure out when everyone was free and when we had a free weekend. My parents loved to go out when we all had a free weekend. When Claire and I were not at a softball field or a basketball court or hanging out with our friends. When my older brother, Nick, was not working or on a family vacation with his best friend. Or when my older sister, Elias, was also not working or back from college or hanging out with her friends. The time that we found that all six of us were free and could hang out as a family is always used to its fullest potential. It usually would consist of having a nice family movie night at home where my mom would pick some movie that she thought we all would enjoy and we all would watch it. While my dad is baking some sort of sweet treat that we all will devour in seconds once he says they are done. Other times the family time consists of going out to a restaurant and having a nice family dinner outing, most likely at Red Robin, The Trappe, Smokin’ Bettys, or Ralph’s in the city. The dinner outings will usually turn into going somewhere to shop like the Philadelphia Premium Outlets or the King of Prussia Mall to get either Christmas gifts or for back-to-school shopping.

annotations.comment: Your details are so good. This essay is authentic and engaging. I'm impressed :)
annotations.context_left: Red Robin, The Trappe, Smokin’ Bettys, or Ralph’s in the city. The dinner outings will usually

paragraphs.body: On a nice beautiful sunny day in July, my family and I were driving to Massanutten in Virginia where we had a timeshare. It was another year of a jam-packed family vacation ahead of us. The first thing we did once we got all settled into our small little cottage in the mountains with a view of the lake from our balcony. We went to our favorite restaurant which was thirty minutes away from our resort. The restaurant was on the campus of James Madison University and was called O’Neills Grill. The restaurant was packed and on all the TVs, different sports games were playing like a Phillies vs Mets game on the same TV and then a Dodgers vs Angels game on another. Other TV’s played poker and others played the local news. Everywhere around you, you could smell the fresh food being served to people. Eavesdropping on the different conversations around you happening at each table. People are stalking what other people are getting for food. Once at the restaurant we sat down at our table and started to discuss the many amazing activities my mother and father had planned for us to do in the following week. The activities that intrigued Claire and me were the zip lining on the mountain on the resort and the “snow tubbing” on the mountain. The conversation never ended with talking about the randomest stuff and at the end of our meal the waitress handed my dad the receipt which would be the first of many of that week.

annotations.text: handed my dad the receipt which would be the first of many of that week.

paragraphs.body: It was another year of going to Disney for vacation which we all were ecstatic about because we all loved Disney and have shared so many memories there. This year was going to be no different. We got up at four a.m. to get to the Philadelphia airport around five to get on our flight at seven. Once landed in the Orlando Airport the smell of sunny Florida came bursting through the windows when driving on the highway to get to the parks. Entering Magic Kingdom feels like home and is a warm comforting feeling that we all share. The smell of cotton candy and the sound of happy children fill our souls as we make our way to the first ride of the day, The Many Adventures of Winnie the Pooh. Seeing the purple honey cart come towards us my mom, Claire and I get all excited to hop into the cart and start singing the songs that we all know by heart. By the end of the night, we make our way to Cosmic Rays to get our chicken fingers and burgers for dinner after a long and successful day in the park. And with that, another receipt gets added to the pile of happy memories that we shared as a family.

annotations.text: And with that, another receipt gets added to the pile of happy memories that we shared as a family.

annotations.comment: I'm so taken with how good this writing is! Just keep your verb tenses consistent (stay in past tense).
annotations.context_left: feels like home and is a warm comforting feeling that we all share. The smell of cotton candy

paragraphs.body: The family outings and adventures always ended with multiple receipts being handed to our parents from either the restaurant or the multiple different shops we went to that day to get whatever we decided that we might need. The family outings sometimes would drag on for too long sometimes and ended up with us four kids starting to bicker with each other more than usual. So our parents would have to figure out a way to distract us and make us stop fighting with each other. That would usually call for my dad to go out and get us all a little snack most likely if we were at the mall or the outlets would be Auntie Anne’s pretzels. So by the end of our adventures, we all would see Auntie Anne’s receipt and remember the time that we started to fight with each other, and our dad would have to go and buy us food to shut us up and make us happy.

annotations.text: So by the end of our adventures, we all would see Auntie Anne’s receipt and remember the time that we started to fight with each other, and our dad would have to go and buy us food to shut us up and make us happy.

paragraphs.body: Another family vacation planned for Disney including my mother's mom and sister who has a family of her own including her husband and son joined us. It was the second to last day of our vacation and my Nana’s birthday and we were going to her favorite park, Hollywood Studios to celebrate her. When we arrived we all got off the bus and waited for my Nana to get off the bus but it did not go as planned. She had fallen off the bus and had to be rushed to the hospital. Disney members constantly made sure that we were good and constantly asked us if we needed anything. Hearing the ambulance coming to get my Nana and hearing my older sister crying on the phone to one of our aunts chilled me. The whole day became a blur but the only thing that I could remember was going to our favorite restaurant in Hollywood called Mama MelRose, this little Italian restaurant near all the Muppets attractions. And like always a receipt came with that meal but this receipt did not remind us of a fun memory, it reminded us of a sad memory that we all shared.

annotations.text: And like always a receipt came with that meal but this receipt did not remind us of a fun memory, it reminded us of a sad memory that we all shared.

paragraphs.body: The receipts were never meant to be the focus of the time we spent together. The receipts were always just the thing to remind us of what we did and what memories were made. The receipts hold the love that we have for each other as a family and will endlessly hold the love that we all share. The memories that we made on these adventures were always the main goal of family time and the new experiences that we all experienced together. The receipts are just the physical things that remind us about what we did. While the memories are always going to be there in our minds forever, sometimes we might forget about them until we see the receipt that reminds us of the quality family time we all experienced together.

annotations.comment: What a fab use of definition paragraphs! These are great. Did you know you were defining the receipts? And that you probably didn't need the last paragraph at all because these paragraphs did all that work for you?
annotations.context_left: physical things that remind us about what we did. While the memories are always going to be

paragraphs.body: However, the receipts can remind us of memories that were not all sunshine and happiness. The receipts sometimes remind us of the hardship that we all have shared. The hardships that we all got through because we are a family and care deeply about each other and want to constantly make sure that all of us are happy. The receipts can sometimes bring up memories that we would rather not remember right away or the memories that we do not immediately think about when we think about memories. The receipts even though are wasteful never have a true purpose in the overall scheme of life besides reminding you of how much money you just spent. They remind me of all the time I have spent with people that I truly love and truly care about. They remind me of everything that we have been through even if it is getting Wawa dinner for Christmas dinner one year because my Nana was in the hospital and it was the only thing that was open on our drive home. Or if it reminds me of going to Tennessee for the first time and going to the Grand Ole Opry and standing in the circle where every famous person who has performed there stood. The receipts, even though they may seem pathetic to some people, mean something to me and remind me of all the amazing adventures I have experienced and have shared with my family.

A family is a group of people who are always there for each other no matter what is going on in their lives. A family is not a group of people who do not support each other. A family is not a singular person who does not care about anyone else. A family is not a group of people who only use each other to get what they want and then throw them away when their purpose is done. A family is not a group of people who do not understand the meaning behind love and caring for others. A family is not a group of people who do not just take each other for granted. A family is a group of people who care and love each other and know what it means to be with each other even if it is just in silence. A family is a group of people who will do anything for each other and sacrifice whatever they have for each other. A family is a group of people who will always accept you for who you are and nothing else and stand with you. A family is a group of people who will share with you those moments of upset and darkness but also those moments of cheeriness and happiness.

annotations.comment: Wow!! What an essay. This is so good. Your details are magic. :) I've made comments in-text, but, overall: way to go!!
